etymology.description: The word 金竹果 (jīn zhú guǒ) combines references to color, form, and nature. 金(金) (jīn, "gold") indicates a golden color; 竹果(竹果) (zhú guǒ, "jinzhu fruit") refers directly to a specific fruit, though details about it might be more colloquial or regional knowledge.
etymology.interpretation: 金(金) (jīn, "gold") suggests the golden color of the fruit, possibly its appearance or value. The term 竹果(竹果) (zhú guǒ, "jinzhu fruit") is a direct reference to a specific fruit; however, in this context, it serves primarily to name the fruit rather than describe it.
